title: Canonical Campaign Truth Model — Event-Sourced Durable State
consumer_purpose: Every campaign and lane state survives app restart and is reconstructable from replayed events. Agents query a context packet projection, not raw state. UI and analytics are derived projections, not direct table reads.
claim_atoms: All campaign.* and lane.* event names registered in EventName (18 new names)
RuntimeEvent schema extended with optional campaignId, laneId, role fields
runtime_events Drizzle table extended with campaign_id, lane_id, role columns
CampaignEvent typed schema defined as RuntimeEvent subset with campaign context
Secretary.writeEvent creates a RuntimeEvent before mutating in-memory state (write-through)
Secretary.init replays all campaign events from EventStore on startup to reconstruct lane state
CampaignProjector service derives CampaignStateProjection from replayed events
CampaignContextPacket format defined as projection: campaign summary + lane state + recent events
CampaignState derived via pure reducer (existing reduceCampaignState) from event stream
Binder events also flow through CampaignEvent → EventStore for durability
Existing predicate engine and state machine unchanged — they consume the same event types
Zero new tables: campaign events share runtime_events table with session events
content:
{
  "schema_version": "v1",
  "plan_id": "in-001-canonical-campaign-truth-model",
  "plan_revision": 2,
  "title": "Canonical Campaign Truth Model — Event-Sourced Durable State",
  "boundary": "packages/opencode/src/campaign/ + packages/opencode/src/event/",
  "consumer_purpose": "Every campaign and lane state survives app restart and is reconstructable from replayed events. Agents query a context packet projection, not raw state. UI and analytics are derived projections, not direct table reads.",
  "claim_atoms": [
    "All campaign.* and lane.* event names registered in EventName (18 new names matching types.ts LaneState)",
    "RuntimeEvent schema extended with optional campaignId, laneId, role fields",
    "runtime_events Drizzle table extended with campaign_id, lane_id, role columns + 2 indexes",
    "CampaignEvent typed schema defined as RuntimeEvent subset with campaign context + helpers",
    "Secretary.unifies to canonical LANE_STATE_MACHINE from state-machine.ts (16 states, not 9)",
    "Secretary.writeEvent creates a RuntimeEvent before mutating in-memory state (write-through)",
    "Secretary.init replays all campaign events through reduceCampaignState on startup",
    "CampaignProjector service derives CampaignStateProjection from replayed events",
    "CampaignContextPacket format defined as agent-facing projection: campaign + lanes + events",
    "Binder events flow through CampaignEvent to EventStore (typed, not ad-hoc literals)",
    "Three RuntimeEvent types unified: secretary _tag union → CampaignEvent → EventStore flat row",
    "Zero new tables: campaign events share runtime_events table with session events"
  ],
  "content": "# IN-001: Canonical Campaign Truth Model

## Root Causes

### RC-1 (HIGH): Secretary state is in-memory only, lost on restart
The Secretary service at `campaign/secretary.ts` manages lane state via `Ref.make(new Map<string, LaneState>())`. The make function in the Layer creates an empty map every time. On app restart, all lane state is gone.

**Evidence:** secretary.ts:252 shows `const map = yield* Ref.make(new Map<string, LaneState>())` with zero persistence logic. No EventStore dependency in the layer.

### RC-2 (HIGH): No campaign event names in EventName registry
The EventName registry at `event/event-names.ts` has 43 names (session.*, permission.*, file.*, etc.) but zero campaign.* or lane.* names.

**Evidence:** Only `CoordSubagentPhase: 'coord.subagent.phase'` relates to multi-agent coordination.

### RC-3 (HIGH): RuntimeEvent schema has no campaign identification fields
The RuntimeEvent schema at `event/runtime-event.ts` has sessionId and runId but no campaignId or laneId. Campaign events can't be attributed to a campaign or lane.

### RC-4 (HIGH): Two competing state machine definitions — secretary vs canonical
The secretary (secretary.ts:146-209) uses a private inline `reduceState` with 9 states (`idle`, `learning`, `planning`, `reviewing`, `executing`, `validating`, `repairing`, `completed`, `failed`, `blocked`). The canonical spec at `state-machine.ts:92-190` defines `LANE_STATE_MACHINE` with 16 states (`created`, `scouting`, `scoped`, `planning`, `critic_review`, `approved`, `executing`, `validating`, `red_team`, `repairing`, `historian`, `checkpointed`, `returned`, `blocked`, `failed`). The types.ts LaneState Schema matches the canonical spec, not the secretary. The secretary never imports state-machine.ts. There is no reconciliation path.

**Evidence:** secretary.ts line 1 imports `{ Context, Effect, Layer, Ref, Schema }` only — no state-machine.ts import. state-machine.ts line 393 `reduceCampaignState` is never called anywhere.

### RC-5 (HIGH): Three structurally incompatible RuntimeEvent types with no mapping
- Secretary.ts:19-34: discriminated `_tag` union with 14 heterogeneous variants (`{ _tag: "LaneCreated" }`, `{ _tag: "LearningComplete"; artifacts: string[] }`, etc.)
- event/runtime-event.ts:9-30: flat 21-field Schema.Struct with `eventType: string` + `payloadJson?: unknown`
- campaign/types.ts:85-92: `{ type: string, timestamp: number, data?: Record<string, unknown> }` — used in binder.executionEvents

No encoder/decoder pair exists between any two of these three types.

### RC-6 (MEDIUM): No startup replay mechanism for campaign events
Even if campaign events were stored, there is no code that replays them on Secretary initialization to reconstruct lane state.

### RC-7 (MEDIUM): No standardized context packet format for agents
Agents have no structured "where are we in this campaign?" projection.

### RC-8 (LOW): Binder uses ad-hoc event type string literals instead of EventName
binder.ts records events with hardcoded `"binder.created"`, `"binder.evidence_added"`, `"binder.status_changed"`, `"binder.finalized"` — bypassing the EventName registry entirely.


## Design

### A. Event Name Registry Expansion (event-names.ts)

Add 18 canonical event names matching the canonical 16-state LaneState from types.ts:

**campaign.* lifecycle (8):**
```
CampaignCreated: 'campaign.created'
CampaignPhaseChanged: 'campaign.phase.changed'
CampaignLaneAdded: 'campaign.lane.added'
CampaignIntegrationStarted: 'campaign.integration.started'
CampaignIntegrationCompleted: 'campaign.integration.completed'
CampaignPushed: 'campaign.pushed'
CampaignCompleted: 'campaign.completed'
CampaignFailed: 'campaign.failed'
```

**lane.* lifecycle (10):**
```
LaneCreated: 'lane.created'
LaneStateChanged: 'lane.state.changed'
LaneRoleStarted: 'lane.role.started'
LaneRoleCompleted: 'lane.role.completed'
LaneCheckpointPublished: 'lane.checkpoint.published'
LaneValidationPassed: 'lane.validation.passed'
LaneValidationFailed: 'lane.validation.failed'
LaneRedTeamFinding: 'lane.redteam.finding'
LaneRepairNeeded: 'lane.repair.needed'
LaneRepairCompleted: 'lane.repair.completed'
```

Total: 18 new constants. All follow existing PascalCase convention.

### B. RuntimeEvent Schema Extension (runtime-event.ts)

Add 3 optional fields to the existing Schema.Struct:
```typescript
export const RuntimeEvent = Schema.Struct({
  // ... 21 existing fields unchanged ...
  campaignId: Schema.optional(Schema.String),
  laneId: Schema.optional(Schema.String),
  role: Schema.optional(Schema.String),
})
```

Why optional: Campaign events are a subset of all RuntimeEvents. Session events don't need these fields. Making them optional avoids breaking every existing consumer.

### C. CampaignEvent Schema (campaign/campaign-event.ts) — NEW FILE

```typescript
import { Schema } from "effect"
import { RuntimeEvent } from "../event/runtime-event"
import { RoleType, CampaignState } from "./types"

/**
 * CampaignEvent is a RuntimeEvent that carries campaign context.
 * It reuses the same runtime_events table via the optional fields
 * added to RuntimeEvent. This type asserts they're present.
 */
export const CampaignEvent = Schema.Struct({
  ...RuntimeEvent.fields,
  campaignId: Schema.String,      // required (overrides optional)
  laneId: Schema.optional(Schema.String),
  role: Schema.optional(RoleType),
  campaignPhase: Schema.optional(CampaignState),
})
export type CampaignEvent = Schema.Schema.Type<typeof CampaignEvent>

/** Helper: tag a RuntimeEvent with campaign context. */
export function toCampaignEvent(
  event: RuntimeEvent,
  campaignId: string,
  opts?: { laneId?: string; role?: string },
): RuntimeEvent {
  return { ...event, campaignId, ...opts }
}

/**
 * Bridge: convert the secretary's discriminated _tag event into
 * a flat RuntimeEvent suitable for EventStore.record().
 * This is THE mapping between the two incompatible RuntimeEvent types.
 */
export function secretaryEventToRuntimeEvent(
  tag: string,
  payload: Record<string, unknown>,
  campaignId: string,
  laneId: string,
): RuntimeEvent {
  // Currently a simple map; will grow as more tag types are added
  return {
    id: crypto.randomUUID(),
    sessionId: campaignId,    // campaign acts as session for event attribution
    runId: laneId,
    ts: new Date().toISOString(),
    actor: "lifecycle",
    eventType: toCanonicalEventName(tag),  // maps _tag → EventName
    phase: "campaign",
    status: "started",
    campaignId,
    laneId,
    payloadJson: payload,
  }
}

/** Map secretary _tag → canonical EventName. */
function toCanonicalEventName(tag: string): string {
  const map: Record<string, string> = {
    LaneCreated: "lane.created",
    StartLearning: "campaign.lane.added",
    Blocked: "lane.state.changed",
    // ... grows as secretary is refactored to use canonical state machine
  }
  return map[tag] ?? "lane.state.changed"
}

export function campaignContextFromPayload(payload: unknown): {
  campaignId?: string; laneId?: string; role?: string
} {
  const p = (payload ?? {}) as Record<string, unknown>
  return {
    campaignId: p.campaignId as string | undefined,
    laneId: p.laneId as string | undefined,
    role: p.role as string | undefined,
  }
}
```

### D. Secretary Refactor: Unify State Machine + Write-Through (secretary.ts)

**This is the most significant change.** Two things happen:

**D1. Replace private `reduceState` with `LANE_STATE_MACHINE` from state-machine.ts**

The secretary's private 9-state `reduceState` function is deleted. It imports and uses `LANE_STATE_MACHINE` from `./state-machine` and `reduceCampaignState` for deterministic state reduction.

Before:
```typescript
// secretary.ts:146-209 — private, 9 states, never changes
type StateTag = "idle" | "learning" | "planning" | ...  // 9 states
function reduceState(current: StateTag, event: RuntimeEvent): StateTag | null { ... }
```

After:
```typescript
import { LANE_STATE_MACHINE, reduceCampaignState } from "./state-machine"
// Uses LANE_STATE_MACHINE.transitions. Current state type is LaneState from types.ts (16 states)
```

**D2. Write-through + startup replay**

Layer signature changes:
```typescript
export const layer: Layer.Layer<Service, never, EventStore.Service | DatabaseAdapter.Service>
```

Startup phase (in `make`):
```typescript
const make = Effect.gen(function* () {
  const store = yield* EventStore.Service
  const map = yield* Ref.make(new Map<string, LaneState>())

  // REPLAY: Read all campaign events from EventStore
  const events = yield* store.query({ eventType: "*", limit: 10000 /* paginate */ })
  for (const event of events) {
    const ctx = campaignContextFromPayload(event.payloadJson)
    if (!ctx.campaignId || !ctx.laneId) continue
    // Reconstruct lane state via canonical reducer
    const lane = map.get(ctx.laneId)
    const laneState: CampaignState = lane ?? { currentState: "created", events: [] }
    const newState = reduceCampaignState(laneState, {
      type: event.eventType,
      timestamp: new Date(event.ts).getTime(),
      data: event.payloadJson as Record<string, unknown>,
    })
    map.set(ctx.laneId, newState)
  }
  // ... rest of implementation
})
```

Write-through in `processEvent`:
```typescript
const processEvent: Interface["processEvent"] = (laneId, event) =>
  Effect.gen(function* () {
    const store = yield* EventStore.Service
    const current = yield* ensureLane(laneId)
    // Map through canonical reducer
    const newState = reduceCampaignState(current, event)
    // 1. Record event first (write-through for durability)
    const runtimeEvent = secretaryEventToRuntimeEvent(
      event._tag, event, current.campaignId, laneId
    )
    yield* store.record(runtimeEvent)
    // 2. Then update in-memory Ref for fast hot-path reads
    yield* Ref.update(map, (m) => { m.set(laneId, newState); return new Map(m) })
  })
```

### E. CampaignEvent Table + Store Changes

**event.sql.ts** — Add 3 columns + 3 indexes:
```typescript
export const RuntimeEventTable = sqliteTable(
  "runtime_events",
  {
    // ... 21 existing columns unchanged ...
    campaign_id: text(),
    lane_id: text(),
    role: text(),
  },
  (table) => [
    // ... 7 existing indexes unchanged ...
    index("runtime_events_campaign_idx").on(table.campaign_id),
    index("runtime_events_lane_idx").on(table.lane_id),
    index("runtime_events_campaign_lane_idx").on(table.campaign_id, table.lane_id),
  ],
)
```

**event-store.ts** — Encode/decode:
- `encodeForDb`: add `campaign_id: event.campaignId ?? null`, `lane_id: event.laneId ?? null`, `role: event.role ?? null`
- `decodeFromDb`: add `campaignId: (row.campaign_id as string) ?? undefined`, etc.
- `QueryFilters`: add optional `campaignId`, `laneId` fields for targeted queries

### F. Binder Event Cleanup (binder.ts)

Replace ad-hoc string literals with EventName references:
```typescript
import { EventName } from "../event/event-names"

// Before: yield* recordBinderEvent(eventStore, "binder.created", laneId, ...)
// After: yield* recordBinderEvent(eventStore, EventName.LaneCreated, laneId, ...)
```

All four hardcoded literals (`"binder.created"`, `"binder.evidence_added"`, `"binder.status_changed"`, `"binder.finalized"`) become canonical EventName values. The `recordBinderEvent` function also encodes campaign context into payloadJson.

### G. Campaign State Projector (campaign/projector.ts) — NEW FILE

An Effect service that replays events to derive current campaign state. Uses `reduceCampaignState` from state-machine.ts as its reducer.

```typescript
export interface CampaignProjection {
  id: string
  goal: string
  phase: CampaignState
  laneCount: number
  lanes: LaneProjection[]
  currentEvents: CampaignEvent[]
  transitionCount: number
  metadata: Record<string, unknown>
  retryBudgets: Record<string, number>
}

export interface LaneProjection {
  id: string
  campaignId: string
  currentState: LaneState
  roleInstances: Role[]
  binderDigest: string
  recentEvents: CampaignEvent[]
}

export interface Interface {
  readonly getCampaignState: (campaignId: string) => Effect.Effect<Option<CampaignProjection>>
  readonly getLaneState: (laneId: string) => Effect.Effect<Option<LaneProjection>>
  readonly getActiveCampaigns: () => Effect.Effect<CampaignProjection[]>
}

export class Service extends Context.Service<Service, Interface>()("@opencode/CampaignProjector") {}
export const use = serviceUse(Service)
```

The projector subscribes to the Bus for campaign events to maintain a hot cache, and falls back to EventStore replay on cold start. Its layer depends on `EventStore.Service | Bus.Service`.

### H. Context Packet Format (campaign/context-packet.ts) — NEW FILE

Standardized agent-facing projection of where the campaign is:

```typescript
export interface CampaignContextPacket {
  campaign: {
    id: string
    goal: string
    phase: CampaignState
    terminalStatus?: TerminalStatus
  }
  lanes: Array<{
    id: string
    scope: string
    state: LaneState
    currentRole?: RoleType
    binder: {
      missionObjective: string
      approvedPlan?: string
      checkpointCommit?: string
      validationResults: string[]
      redTeamFindings: string[]
    }
  }>
  recentEvents: Array<{
    type: string
    ts: string
    role?: string
    status?: string
  }>
  pendingTransitions: Array<{
    from: string
    to: string
    reason: string
  }>
  integrationStatus?: {
    plan: IntegrationPlan
    conflicts: number
    mergeStrategy: string
  }
}
```

Served via a `campaign_context` Tool in agent-queries.ts.


## Files Changed

### New files (3):
| File | Lines | Responsibility |
|---|---|---|
| `campaign/campaign-event.ts` | ~90 | CampaignEvent schema, secretary event bridge, toCanonicalEventName mapper |
| `campaign/projector.ts` | ~150 | CampaignProjector Effect service + projections |
| `campaign/context-packet.ts` | ~80 | Context packet format + builder function |

### Modified files (6):
| File | Key change | Scope |
|---|---|---|
| `event/event-names.ts` | +18 constants | Add campaign.* + lane.* names |
| `event/runtime-event.ts` | +3 optional fields | campaignId, laneId, role |
| `event/event.sql.ts` | +3 columns + 3 indexes | Drizzle migration |
| `event/event-store.ts` | +6 encode/decode lines + 2 filter fields | Encoder + QueryFilters |
| `campaign/secretary.ts` | **Major:** unify state machine, write-through, replay | ~100 lines changed |
| `campaign/binder.ts` | +4 EventName references | Replace literals |
| `event/agent-queries.ts` | +1 Tool definition | CampaignContextTool |


## Execution Plan (5 Waves)

### Wave A: Schema + Registry (parallel — no dependency between these 5 files)
1. **event-names.ts** — Add 18 campaign.* and lane.* event names
2. **runtime-event.ts** — Add 3 optional fields to Schema.Struct
3. **event.sql.ts** — Add 3 columns + 3 indexes
4. **event-store.ts** — Encode/decode new fields; add campaignId/laneId to QueryFilters
5. **campaign/campaign-event.ts** — CREATE: CampaignEvent schema, secretaryEventToRuntimeEvent bridge, toCanonicalEventName mapper

### Wave B: State Machine Unification (depends on Wave A schema existing for type safety)
6. **campaign/secretary.ts** — Replace private `reduceState` with imports of `LANE_STATE_MACHINE` and `reduceCampaignState` from `./state-machine`. Delete the 9-state enum. Add `EventStore.Service` to layer dependencies.

### Wave C: Durability (depends on Wave B)
7. **campaign/secretary.ts** (continued) — Add write-through in processEvent: call `store.record()` via `secretaryEventToRuntimeEvent` before Ref.update. Add startup replay in `make`: query EventStore, replay through `reduceCampaignState`, populate Ref.
8. **campaign/binder.ts** — Replace 4 ad-hoc string literals with EventName references; encode campaign context in payloadJson.

### Wave D: Projection (depends on Wave A)
9. **campaign/projector.ts** — CREATE: CampaignProjector service with hot cache + cold replay
10. **campaign/context-packet.ts** — CREATE: Context packet format + builder

### Wave E: Agent Tooling (depends on Wave D)
11. **event/agent-queries.ts** — Add CampaignContextTool


## Risk Assessment

| Risk | Severity | Mitigation |
|---|---|---|
| Adding fields to RuntimeEvent breaks consumers that spread the type | HIGH | Fields are `Schema.optional`. Spread consumers pick up undefined fields without TypeScript errors. |
| Deleting secretary's private `reduceState` breaks its internal 14-event logic | HIGH | The canonical `LANE_STATE_MACHINE` has 16 states — needs mapping of secretary's 9 states to the canonical 16. This is a one-time mapping function. Pre-existing secretary events (stored in `eventStream: []`) will have `_tag` values that map to canonical names via `toCanonicalEventName`. |
| secretaryEventToRuntimeEvent bridge is incomplete — 14 _tag variants | MEDIUM | Map is incremental. Unknown tags default to `lane.state.changed`. As secretary refactors to use canonical state machine, fewer tags need bridging. |
| Startup replay on large event stores is slow | MEDIUM | Add composite index on (campaign_id, lane_id). Query only campaign-scoped events. Add limit/pagination. |
| Write-through doubles latency for processEvent | LOW | DB write via DatabaseAdapter is async-over-sync. Ref update is synchronous after write. ~1-2ms added. |
| Existing tests for secretary fail after refactor | MEDIUM | Tests need a FakeEventStore stub. The secretary's unit tests test state transitions — these should work with the canonical state machine directly. |
| Binder literal replacement misses a string | LOW | Grep for all string literals containing 'binder.' and verify each maps to EventName. |


## Validation Strategy

### Wave A validation
```bash

rg 'Campaign.*:.*campaign\.' packages/opencode/src/event/event-names.ts | wc -l

rg 'Lane.*:.*lane\.' packages/opencode/src/event/event-names.ts | wc -l

rg 'laneId' packages/opencode/src/event/runtime-event.ts

rg 'runtime_events_campaign_lane_idx' packages/opencode/src/event/event.sql.ts

# 4. TypeScript check
cd packages/opencode && bun typecheck
```

### Wave B validation
```bash

rg 'LANE_STATE_MACHINE|reduceCampaignState|state-machine' packages/opencode/src/campaign/secretary.ts

# 6. Verify private reduceState is gone
rg 'function reduceState' packages/opencode/src/campaign/secretary.ts || echo "REMOVED OK"

# 7. TypeScript check

```

### Wave C validation
```bash

rg 'store\.record' packages/opencode/src/campaign/secretary.ts

# 9. Verify startup replay: EventStore query in make function

rg '"binder\.' packages/opencode/src/campaign/binder.ts || echo "CLEAN OK"

# 12. TypeScript check
cd packages/opencode && bun typecheck
```

### Full validation
```bash
# 13. Run all campaign tests
cd packages/opencode && bun test --filter campaign

# 14. Run full typecheck
cd packages/opencode && bun typecheck
```


## Rollback Plan (with dirty file preservation)

```bash
# Step 1: Preserve dirty state from files we modified
MODIFIED=(
  packages/opencode/src/event/event-names.ts
  packages/opencode/src/event/runtime-event.ts
  packages/opencode/src/event/event.sql.ts
  packages/opencode/src/event/event-store.ts
  packages/opencode/src/campaign/secretary.ts
  packages/opencode/src/campaign/binder.ts
  packages/opencode/src/event/agent-queries.ts
)
git stash push -m "pre-rollback-in-001" -- "${MODIFIED[@]}"

# Step 2: Remove new files
rm packages/opencode/src/campaign/campaign-event.ts
rm packages/opencode/src/campaign/projector.ts
rm packages/opencode/src/campaign/context-packet.ts

# Step 3: Checkout to clean state
git checkout -- "${MODIFIED[@]}"

# Step 4: Restore original dirty state
git stash pop
```


## Architecture Diagram

```
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                      Event Store (runtime_events table)                  │
│  sessionId | campaignId | laneId | eventType | payloadJson | ts | ...   │
└─────────┬───────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────┬───────┘
          │                   │                                    │
   session projector    campaign projector                   duckdb projector
          │                   │                                    │
   session state     CampaignProjection                      analytics views
   (messages,        (lanes, phases, roles,                  (trends, hotspots,
    status)           binder digests)                        error summaries)
                          │
                     Context Packet
                     (agent-facing projection)

Write path:
  Secretary.processEvent
    → secretaryEventToRuntimeEvent (bridge _tag → EventName)
      → EventStore.record (durable write)
        → Ref.update (hot cache)

Read path (cold start):
  EventStore.query({ campaignId })
    → reduceCampaignState (pure reducer from state-machine.ts)
      → CampaignProjection

Read path (hot):
  Bus.subscribe(campaign.*)
    → reduceCampaignState (incremental)
      → Updated CampaignProjection

Current state (broken):
  Ref<Map> ← private reduceState — LOST ON RESTART

Target state:
  EventStore ← secretaryEventToRuntimeEvent ← secretary ← LANE_STATE_MACHINE
  EventStore → reduceCampaignState → CampaignProjector → Context Packet → Agent
```


## Appendix: State Machine Reconciliation

### Secretary's private states (9 — DELETE):
`idle`, `learning`, `planning`, `reviewing`, `executing`, `validating`, `repairing`, `completed`, `failed`, `blocked`

### Canonical LaneState from types.ts (16 — USE):
`created`, `scouting`, `scoped`, `planning`, `critic_review`, `approved`, `executing`, `validating`, `red_team`, `repairing`, `historian`, `checkpointed`, `returned`, `blocked`, `failed`

### Mapping (secretary → canonical):
| Secretary 9-state | Canonical 16-state | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| idle | created | Initial state |
| learning | scouting + scoped | Learning becomes 2 canonical states |
| planning | planning | Direct mapping |
| reviewing | critic_review + approved | Review splits into review + approval |
| executing | executing | Direct mapping |
| validating | validating | Direct mapping |
| (missing) | red_team | New state, no secretary equivalent |
| repairing | repairing | Direct mapping |
| (missing) | historian | New state — final report phase |
| (missing) | checkpointed | New state — checkpoint published |
| (missing) | returned | New state — lane returned for rework |
| completed | (terminal) | Represents completion |
| failed | failed | Direct mapping |
| blocked | blocked | Direct mapping |

This reconciliation is a one-time mapping in `toCanonicalEventName`. The secretary's processEvent will need to handle the finer granularity of the canonical state machine.
",
  "status": "proposed",
  "created_at": "2026-05-30T12:30:00.000Z",
  "modified_at": "2026-05-30T12:30:00.000Z",
  "revision_notes": "Rev 2: Added RC-4 (dual state machine definitions), RC-5 (3 incompatible RuntimeEvent types), RC-8 (binder ad-hoc literals). Added Fix D (state machine unification: delete private reduceState, import LANE_STATE_MACHINE from state-machine.ts). Added secretaryEventToRuntimeEvent bridge between _tag union and EventStore flat row. Added state machine reconciliation appendix. Updated wave plan: Wave B is now state machine unification before durability. Fixed claim_atoms to match canonical 16-state LaneState."
}
revision_reason: Incorporated critical findings from root-cause analysis: (1) dual competing state machine definitions (secretary private reduceState vs canonical LANE_STATE_MACHINE), (2) 3 incompatible RuntimeEvent types with no mapping layer. Added RC-6 and RC-7, added Fix D (state machine unification), updated campaign event names to match the canonical 16-state LaneState from types.ts. Fixed the replay logic to use the canonical state machine, not the secretary's private one.
